title: mine-wait/scan timeouts are unnamed magic numbers
description: In e2e_testnet.rs the receive-side block uses Duration::from_secs(300) and Duration::from_secs(120) directly. PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS is a named constant at the top of the file, but the two sub-budget values are not. The inline comment 'Budget: mine-wait ≤ 300 s, scan ≤ 120 s; together 420 s within PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS' is the only thing keeping the arithmetic correct. If someone changes PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS (e.g. to 400 s) without noticing the inline comment, the combined sub-budget silently exceeds the outer deadline. The 30-second poll interval inside the mine-wait loop is a third unnamed magic number. Fix: add named constants MINE_WAIT_TIMEOUT_SECS, SCAN_TIMEOUT_SECS, and MINE_POLL_INTERVAL_SECS at the top of the file alongside PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS, with a debug_assert that their sum is less than PAYMENT_TIMEOUT_SECS.
